methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a pre-seed stage Infrastructure & Logistics company that has raised $3.4M to date. The comparable universe in this sector is sparse: two private peers (AiDash and Watoga Technologies) exist, but neither operates at the same stage or valuation tier, making narrow peer-median analysis inapplicable. No public comparables exist in the relevant segment.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology provides the primary anchor. Using $12.0B as a total-raised proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to next-stage advancement yields $30.0B implied value. This method is applicable here because it calibrates typical magnitude shifts within frontier-infrastructure investing, though it relies on sparse empirical grounding in this specific sector.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a range of $25.5B to $34.5B with a median of $30.0B. Key uncertainties include: the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for Durin's specific market dynamics, the limited peer set preventing cross-validation, and the absence of revenue or traction metrics to stress-test the valuation. This range should be treated as indicative only pending accumulation of comparable transaction data or material operating milestones.
